summary: The patient requires immediate fluid resuscitation due to hypotension; however, given her pregnant state indicated by the positive urine pregnancy test, close monitoring is essential as she may have placental abruption or other complications necessitating urgent evaluation and potential intervention if signs suggest internal bleeding or severe maternal distress. To ensure safety during management decisions, ruling out serious issues such as uterine rupture remains critical prior to definitive treatment plans. Agent1 recommends prioritizing aggressive hydration but keeping options open based on evolving diagnostic findings indicative possibly towards more invasive procedures depending upon acute presentation changes observed clinically over next few hours after initiating supportive care strategies initially aimed at restoring circulatory stability first via IV fluids administration followed closely thereafter by serial ultrasounds & lab workups focusing especially on hemoglobin levels/descent trends suggestive possible concealed blood loss requiring emergent hysterectomy consideration
